author: jtraglia
content: <@&1417820113981145228> I would appreciate reviews on the EIP-8282 consensus-specs PR. We want to merge this & create a release within the next ~10 hours. https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5359

author: potuz
content: I don't have the rights to unresolve comments I think

author: nflaig
content: what do you want unresolved?

author: potuz
content: left a comment on the issue of using withdrawal credentials to be the builder version, which I think should be unresolved

author: jtraglia
content: Meaning that it's 0x03 instead of 0x00?

author: potuz
content: no, meaning that it allows for anything

author: nflaig
content: I unresolved it

author: jtraglia
content: I understand but I'm not sure that I agree. The way I see it, we should only allow 0x00 or we should allow everything. I don't see a situation in which we ignore a deposit because of something in the withdrawal credentials, other than a bad signature. If someone wants to make a 0x01 builder right now, they just might not be able to participate if the withdrawal creds are interpreted differently, ie a new field in those 11 empty bytes after the version. They would need to exit & re-deposit. For example, it's entirely possible to make a 0x00 validator with an invalid pubkey right now.

author: jtraglia
content: But this reminds me, we should add a check in bid gossip validation that the builder's version is 0.

author: jtraglia
content: And if we wanted, we could say that only 0x00 builders can exit.

author: potuz
content:
Three options, and pros and cons. 

1) Leave as is: builders can create any version right now, so this makes it impossible for the protocol to later use versions to enforce behavior. This effectively makes versions kinda useless.
2) Enforce 0x00 for the first version at the fork. This is better than the above, it doesn't suffer from the above problem.
3) Do not enforce 0x00, but enforce a region. This is the best of both worlds: this doesn't suffer from the problem in 1 but at the same time it allows for offchain protocols to enforce whatever behavior they want on the version (eg a protocol that requires their proposers to only contact builders of version 0x07).

author: jtraglia
content: I would say your assessment of 1 is false. We would just enforce it elsewhere, not at the builder structure. Give me a real example of this that is not just "execution address must be potuz.eth"

author: potuz
content: you cannot enforce it elsewhere? I can send a request to the contract and create that version, how do you enforce it?

author: jtraglia
content: What do you mean? We enforce things with 0x01 and 0x02 validators.

author: jtraglia
content: Eg when consolidating, X must match Y.

author: jtraglia
content: If this is a new builder structure field, we can just set all existing builders with that version to an invalid value.

author: potuz
content: yes but we went with 2 for validators: you could not deposit a validator with withdrawal credentials 0x02 at genesis

author: potuz
content: we didn't go with 1)

author: jtraglia
content: No we didn't. You could always deposit a 0x02 validator. I'm pretty sure.

author: jtraglia
content: To be clear, I'm talking about mainnet during phase0.

author: potuz
content:
can you explain me what the complain is here? why do you feel it's fine to allow any version to be created by builders? If you want a real example that is not send money to potuz.eth, here are a few possible future lines of exploration:

1) Builders of type 0xEF can also validate. 
2) Builders of type 0xEF are required to send their payloads with a ZK proof
3) Builders of type 0xEF get issuance from the protocol, or pay issuance to the protocol..

author: potuz
content: is the problem that adding a single assert to not have a range is too complex? or can lose money to someone that uses non-standard tooling and creates a builder with the reserved domain?

author: jtraglia
content: What is there to enforce here? Why should this be done when adding a builder to the registry?

author: jtraglia
content: We can add those asserts in the functions when necessary? No?

author: potuz
content: the enforcement is that there can't be a builder on the registry with a reserved version, so that when we actually want to use a version, for whatever reason, we are sure that before the fork where the version is allowed, there are no builders with that version. This allows us to use any kind of enforcement of the version, that would be impossible to enforce if builders existed before. Eg: in the future fork where we allow builders to also be post-quantum aggregators, they need to be staked at least 2K ETH, we would need to special case those preexisting builders that frontrun the fork, instead of simply not allowing them before the fork

author: jtraglia
content: In this example, we'd just have a check somewhere that builder.balance is 2048. This wouldn't be enforced at deposit time.

author: jtraglia
content: Eg in `process_pq_aggregate` we'd have checks like `assert builder.version == X` and `assert builder.balance >= 2048`.

author: jtraglia
content: Does that make sense? These things can/should be enforced elsewhere.

author: potuz
content: not necessarily: the logic can assume that the builder always has above 2K in the exact same way that we assume now that the builder has more than 1 **because** we enforced it at deposit time and it's impossible to go below. The whole point is that a single one line with a single assert enables **any** behavior we want in the future, while not having this reservation precludes us from using any behavior we want. So again, I have given you a few arguments why to add it, can you give me a single one why not to add it?

author: potuz
content: notice that I don't mind enfocing 0x00, what I think it´s wrong is just allowing anything

author: jtraglia
content: Can enforce at registry time doesn't mean we *must* enforce there. The three examples you gave above are not convincing lol. Reasons to allow any version: (1) This functions the same as validators do today. (2) If we introduce a new version of builder, we could have those ready at the fork; and not be in this awkward onboarding situation we currently face. (3) Limiting some versions would mean non-consecutive versions, ie a jump from version 0 to version 100. (4) These enforcements can be handled elsewhere.

author: jtraglia
content: I'm not strongly opposed to reserving versions > 0x0F. Just in case we need to do whatever you describe. But also I just do not see us actually using that. But who knows.

author: jtraglia
content: Maybe <@586161934425128960> can weigh in? Happy to do whatever at this point.

author: jtraglia
content: Also, say there does exist a builder with version=0xEF and we need to impose some restrictions. We could just change the builder to version=0 at the fork or forcefully exit them, no?

author: nflaig
content: the reason why I left this comment was along the lines of what <@755590043632140352> is saying

author: nflaig
content: if you have to assert a 2nd conditions, what's the `version` actually useful for? just informational?

author: nflaig
content: but we do the same for validators right now so this might be fine

author: jtraglia
content: It would be the same as 0x01 validators today. The withdrawal credentials can be interpreted differently.

author: jtraglia
content: We do not enforce that the last 20 byte's of their withdrawal credential is a valid eth1 address.

author: jtraglia
content: But we will happily interpret it as an address & send ETH to it.

author: jtraglia
content: <@755590043632140352> <@586161934425128960> how about we just merge what we have and debate this more later. If core devs want to limit the version, we can do that. Not sure this is enough to delay the release & glamsterdam-devnet-6.

author: jtraglia
content: When you have time, please open a PR with the `assert request.withdrawal_credentials[0] & 0xF0 == 0x00` change.

author: nflaig
content: I already approved it, leaving it up to <@755590043632140352>

author: nflaig
content: wouldn't this be a `continue` or `return`?

author: jtraglia
content:
Another PR to review. I hope this isn't controversial. Follow up from the 8282 PR which I said I would do.
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5377

author: potuz
content: 😂 in the first PR I ask you to add an assert and you didn't like it... in this one I ask you to remove an assert, we'll see where we land 😅

author: jtraglia
content: Oh gosh lol.

author: jtraglia
content:
> then off protocol projects may want to use that
If there's a will, there's a way. Nothing we do would prevent this.

author: jtraglia
content: Also why should we care what people do off-protocol?

author: jtraglia
content: And it's not about the assert. It's about what the assert does.

author: potuz
content: yes, and I think the assert in the payload bid processing is also unnecessary

author: jtraglia
content: 😢 why?

author: jtraglia
content: Say we add a new builder version something else in the future, likely to happen.

author: jtraglia
content: For something that wouldn't submit bids. Should they be allowed to submit bids?

author: jtraglia
content: I'm just trying to think ahead a bit. I think it's a good idea to restrict that right now.

author: potuz
content: funny that you think it's alright to restrict in every spot that uses a version, instead of on the single  spot of creation of the builder, that would ensure that all other spots are satisfied.

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah, because that would prevent onboarding of other versions before the fork. And it would burn deposits when accidentally depositing with a 0x03 credential.

author: potuz
content: which is precisely why I am proposing that we simply reserve, in the single spot, some versions. Then you can add extra restrictions if you want on other spots

author: potuz
content: Adding a restriction today, that is not used at all, and allowing them to exist, doesn't make much sense to me.

author: jtraglia
content: But how would that solve the onboarding issue? Say 0x10 - 0xFF is reserved. If we introduce proof builders when mandatory proofs is included in an upgrade. Will it use 0x01 or 0x10?

author: jtraglia
content: They can exist _before_ the fork & participate _after_ the fork.

author: potuz
content: it depends on what we need to enforce: if the new type of builders **requires** that they could not have existed any builder before the fork, then we would use the reserved space, that's precisely the reason to have it. If the new type of builders does not have this requirement, then we can simply use the any index outside of the reserved space. The point of the reservation is to allow us to have more flexibility for unforeseen designs in the future.

author: jtraglia
content: With the 0x10 - 0xFF reservation, we would still keep the version & checks defined in https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5377 ?

author: jtraglia
content: And just have the reservation as a "just in case we need it" thing?

author: potuz
content:
The two things are independent, you can even have two reservation spaces. And 5377 would be equivalent to reserve != 0 for participation and nothing for existence. 

Reserving for participation is restrictive in that if we ever need to onboard a new type of builder that should exist before the next fork and could participate before the next fork, then asserting version == 0 precludes this from working. While if the assertion was version < 10, then we can onboard builders with version 9 for that role

author: potuz
content: Anyway, this is getting out of hand over trivialities, I don't feel that strongly about 5377 as I do about the other one

author: jtraglia
content: I was planning on merging this before making the release, but now I'm not sure if I can...

author: potuz
content: it's ok to merge

author: potuz
content: it's also OK not to add the assertions at all

author: potuz
content: I feel more strongly about the other one being wrong to not reserve a space on the deposit processing, because that completely dissalows us from certain constructions, for no reason

author: jtraglia
content: Alright thanks. I think I'm going to merge it and make the release. I think it's important to set the version to 0 when onboarding. I am open to reserving space for builder versions. Since starting this mini-debate, I have been convinced of its value and would probably support it as long as it doesn't prevent us from onboarding a new version before the fork.
